features.notes: REAL typescript@5.9.3 (_tsc.js ~6MB CJS) runs on the host engine: tsc --version + a .ts→.js compile type-checked against staged lib.*.d.ts (exit 0) + a genuine TS2322 type error (exit 2). 3/3. Exercised the CJS loader at scale + fd-based fs. eslint/prettier/npm next

features.title: zero-copy SAB bundle — workers skip per-spawn disk+decompress
features.notes: host decompresses the node-lib bundle once (cached) into a SharedArrayBuffer, shared zero-copy with every worker (Node+browser); ~14% off Node cold runs (48→41ms). Measured: nodert ~41ms cold = ~68x faster than the VM (~2.8s); bundle transfer negligible (0.36ms) so worker-spawn+boot is the floor. Warm-pool is the next lever (isolation tradeoff)

features.title: engines config — createNano({ engines: { node, routing } })
features.notes: vm/nodert/auto policy + routing pins; nodert host-engine runtime VENDORED into the SDK (src/vendor/nodert, byte-identical mirror gated by check-vendor; copied to dist/vendor on build) and wired to Nano.node() over the shared Kernel/VFS. auto falls back to the VM on ERR_NODERT_UNSUPPORTED or if the runtime is unreachable. Tested: Nano.node('nodert') runs on the host engine through the built dist. Browser worker-bundle path (pass the node-lib bundle in init) is the remaining terminal integration.

features.title: nodert host-engine tier live in the SDK (K9)
features.notes: vendored nodert runs Node on the browser JS engine over the shared Kernel; cross-tier VFS both directions verified headlessly (worker_threads); node-lib bundle ships in dist/vendor/nodert

features.title: nodert browser boot — bundle-in-init (host-loaded, no fs/brotli)
features.notes: host loadLibBundle (Node: disk+brotli / browser: fetch+DecompressionStream gzip via a .gz sibling) passes libIndex/libBytes/fixtures in the worker init; worker boots via initFromBytes. gzip==brotli byte-identity + boot-from-bytes verified headlessly. E2E SCAFFOLD landed: site-root loader candidate + e2e:fixtures stages dist/vendor→public/vendor + harness boot({engines}) + 22-nodert.spec.ts (boots WITHOUT a node ELF so any node output proves the host engine). vite build green; live browser run is CI (test:e2e). Terminal UI toggle remaining

tests/e2e/terminal.spec.ts::shell boots and echoes a typed command

features.title: run wasm32-wasip1 modules as kind:wasm processes
wasi_snapshot_preview1 shim over the Syscall Bus
features.notes: core syscalls (args/env/fd/path/clock/random/exit); fd_readdir + more in W-2

features.title: compiled-Module cache keyed by content hash
WASI service runner — wasm module as a svc.* Kernel Service
features.notes: createWasiService + registerWasmServiceFromManifest: a wasip1 module runs per-invoke as a request→stdin→wasm→stdout→response filter, reachable over the svc.* bus (list/invoke); catalog kind:wasm-service manifest drives registration. runWasm gained stdinBytes. 6/6 headless (stdin-echo fixture). SDK install auto-routing DONE (Nano.installApp auto-registers wasm-services)

features.title: persistent/warm wasm-service — one instance reused across invokes (SWC-style)
features.notes: createWarmWasmService instantiates a compute REACTOR ONCE and reuses it, so memory/state persists across invokes (increment → 1,2,3; reset() → cold); reachable over svc.*, persistent-flagged. 4/4 headless (counter-reactor fixture). Complements the per-invoke command filter

features.title: SDK install auto-routing — Nano.installApp registers a wasm-service
features.notes: installApp auto-registers a kind:wasm-service with the Kernel service registry via the vendored runner (Nano.registerWasmService); wasm-apps untouched. 3/3 headless (dist Nano + real Kernel + Map-backed fs)

features.title: catalog wasm-app / wasm-component artifact kinds
features.notes: Manifest.kind discriminator (elf/wasm-app/service/component) + package.mjs magic-sniff + wasm32-wasip1 abi + manifestKind()/isWasmKind() helpers shipped & tested (both sign/verify sides); wasm-app installs onto PATH (.wasm router runs it); wasm-service registers with the service registry via the WASI service runner (wasm.service.runner). SDK install auto-routing + wasm-component→ServeBridge pending (W-3)
